goal: Deliver the Composer + Reviewer sub-agent vertical slice: identity files, per-role contract templates, supporting skills, and the Effect services (ContextEngine, AgentRunner, EvalParser) plus runTask orchestration, testable end-to-end with an echo-agent fake.
constraints: Services are Effect Context.Tag + Layer pairs, swappable in tests.
All new types under src/types/*.d.ts, ambient (no exports), PascalCase filenames.
Tests under __test__/ folders adjacent to the code they exercise.
No `any`, no `as unknown as`. Errors are tagged unions via effect/Schema.

Subagent spawn command is fixed: claude -p --agent <Composer|Reviewer> --output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions. Model, tools, skills, maxTurns come from the agent frontmatter.
Never spawn with detached: true — prior incident left zombie claude processes. Use default process-group behavior; cancellation via SIGTERM to the direct child pid.
Subagent frontmatter lists skills explicitly. Do not rely on cwd auto-discovery — sub-agents running as main do not inherit skills.
Templating library: handlebars. Per-invocation render, no compile cache in v0.

Log tee paths: .tap/features/<slug>/logs/<task_id>/iter-NNN-<role>.jsonl (stdout) and .stderr.log (stderr). Eval output .tap/features/<slug>/eval/EVAL_RESULT.md is written by Reviewer itself, not by the harness.

This feature does not deliver a FeatureContract service, a LoopRunner scheduler, a CLI, or any VCS / auto-commit behavior. Caller (future LoopRunner) is responsible for contract I/O, scheduling, retries, and VCS policy.

stories.title: Foundation: dependency + shared type declarations
acceptance: `bun install` succeeds with handlebars pinned in package.json.
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes with all new/renamed ambient type files in place.
Task, Story, Feature, TaskResult, EvalIssue, RunTaskError, AgentEvent, and the four service-contract types are globally available without imports.

tasks.title: Add handlebars dependency
acceptance: `handlebars` appears in package.json dependencies.
`bun install` runs clean and updates bun.lock.
`import Handlebars from "handlebars"` resolves under tsc.

tasks.title: Rename existing ambient type files to PascalCase
acceptance: src/types/agent-runner.d.ts is removed and its content lives at src/types/AgentRunner.d.ts.
src/types/loop-runner.d.ts is removed and its content lives at src/types/LoopRunner.d.ts.
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes after rename.

LoopRunner.d.ts wraps declarations in `declare global { ... }` so interfaces remain ambient globals under `moduleDetection: "force"` (the stray `import type { Effect }` otherwise makes the file a module).
Stale `AgentResult` interface is dropped; AgentRunner.d.ts is left as a placeholder populated by S1.T4.
Stale path-import in src/services/LoopRunner.ts is removed (ambient types resolve without module import).

tasks.title: Author Contract.d.ts (Task, Story, Feature) with branded ID + path types
acceptance: Contract.d.ts declares ambient global types Task, Story, Feature matching DESIGN.md §2 shape (Task fields: id, title, files, acceptance, depends_on, status, attempts, maxAttempts; Story: id, title, acceptance, tasks; Feature: feature, goal, constraints, stories).
Status union is exactly `"pending" | "in_progress" | "done" | "failed"`.
Declares a generic `Brand<T, B>` helper and three concrete branded types — `TaskId`, `StoryId`, `AbsolutePath` — globally available to downstream type files.
Task.id is `TaskId`, Task.depends_on is `readonly TaskId[]`, Task.files is `readonly AbsolutePath[]`, Story.id is `StoryId`.
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes.

tasks.title: Extend AgentRunner.d.ts with AgentEvent + run signature
acceptance: AgentEvent is a discriminated union with variants `system`, `assistant`, `user`, `result` carrying the fields listed in SPECS.md <feature:shape>.
ContentBlock union covers `text`, `tool_use`, `tool_result`.

An `AgentRunOptions` type declares { role: "Composer" | "Reviewer"; stdin: string; cwd: AbsolutePath; attempt: number; logPath: AbsolutePath; stderrLogPath: AbsolutePath; evalPath?: AbsolutePath } — path fields use the `AbsolutePath` brand from Contract.d.ts.
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes.

tasks.title: Author RunTask.d.ts (TaskResult, EvalIssue, RunTaskError)
acceptance: EvalIssue fields: acceptanceFailed, file (AbsolutePath), problem, suggestedFix.
TaskResult fields: taskId (TaskId), attempt, verdict, rationale, issues, composerLogPath (AbsolutePath), reviewerLogPath (AbsolutePath), evalResultPath (AbsolutePath), durationMs.
RunTaskError is a tagged union covering exactly the eight variants listed in SPECS.md <feature:shape>; path fields in EvalResultMissing and FilesystemError use the `AbsolutePath` brand.
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes.

tasks.title: Author ContextEngine.d.ts and EvalParser.d.ts
acceptance: ContextEngine.d.ts declares two role-specific input types — ComposerRenderInput { task; feature; specsPath; contractPath; featureRoot; attempt; priorEval: Option.Option<AbsolutePath>; gitStatus } and ReviewerRenderInput { task; feature; specsPath; contractPath; featureRoot; attempt; evalPath: AbsolutePath } — plus TemplateRenderError { _tag: "TemplateRenderFailed"; template: string; missingKey: string }. No `null` or `undefined` in the surface; absence is modeled with `Option` from effect, per project CLAUDE.md rule.
File imports `type { Option } from "effect"` and wraps the declarations in `declare global { ... }` to keep ambient visibility (standard pattern — same as LoopRunner.d.ts).

EvalParser.d.ts declares EvalResult { verdict: "PASS" | "FAIL"; rationale: string; issues: ReadonlyArray<EvalIssue> } and EvalParseError = Extract<RunTaskError, { _tag: "EvalParseFailed" | "EvalResultMissing" }> (aligned with the RunTaskError tagged union via Extract, single source of truth in RunTask.d.ts).
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes.

stories.title: Static content: sub-agent identities, skills, prompt templates
acceptance: Composer and Reviewer sub-agent files exist with the frontmatter + body agreed in the interview.
Three skill files exist with the bodies agreed in the interview.
Two prompt templates exist with handlebars placeholders matching the RenderInput shape.
A dry parse of each markdown file succeeds: YAML frontmatter is valid, handlebars compiles.

tasks.title: Author .claude/agents/Composer.md
acceptance: Frontmatter has: name: Composer, description (single sentence), model: sonnet, skills: [tdd, anti-patterns], maxTurns: 50.
Body includes the rules listed in SPECS: no files outside task.files, no commits/pushes, must run bun test + bunx tsc --noEmit before exit, must read prior_eval_path on retry.
YAML frontmatter parses cleanly with a standard parser.

tasks.title: Author .claude/agents/Reviewer.md
acceptance: Frontmatter has: name: Reviewer, description (single sentence), model: opus, skills: [anti-patterns, code-review], maxTurns: 50.
Body forbids editing source code or committing, requires independent re-run of bun test + bunx tsc --noEmit, and names EVAL_RESULT.md (at the supplied eval_path) as the only file Reviewer may write.
YAML frontmatter parses cleanly.

tasks.title: Author .claude/skills/tdd/SKILL.md
acceptance: Frontmatter has a `description` field that explicitly mentions the trigger condition (acceptance referring to a test file).
Body describes the red-green-refactor loop, one-assertion-per-test rule, behavior-named tests, small-fast-unit preference.
Content is code-agnostic (no Bun-, TypeScript-, or Effect-specific rules).
Skill is discoverable by the Claude Code harness (appears in the registered skills list).

tasks.title: Author .claude/skills/anti-patterns/SKILL.md
acceptance: Frontmatter description explains the trigger (writing or reviewing code before finalizing).
Body enumerates at minimum: monolithic files (>~300 lines), 3+ duplication rule, no impurity in pure zones, deep-nesting smell, magic values, vague names, commented-out code, implicit contracts.
Content is code-agnostic.
Skill is discoverable by the Claude Code harness.

tasks.title: Author .claude/skills/code-review/SKILL.md
acceptance: Frontmatter description identifies the trigger: acting as Reviewer in the tap-tool Ralph loop.

Body covers: per-criterion classification (Satisfied / Not satisfied / Partial), zero-trust verification steps, scope-check (nothing touched outside task.files), verdict rules (PASS only when every criterion satisfied + tests green + no anti-pattern violations).
Body references the <eval:verdict> schema but defers full emission details to REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md.
Skill is discoverable by the Claude Code harness.

tasks.title: Author .tap/prompts/COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md
acceptance: Template contains handlebars placeholders for: feature_goal, feature_constraints, task_id, task_title, task_files, task_acceptance, specs_path, contract_path, prior_eval_path, git_status.
Template ends with an explicit kickoff directive (`## Begin now.` or equivalent).

Template uses triple-brace `{{{ }}}` for every placeholder that may carry user-authored text with `{{` in it (feature_goal, feature_constraints items, task_title, task_files items, task_acceptance items, git_status); double-brace is reserved for controlled IDs/paths (task_id, specs_path, contract_path).
`prior_eval_path` is wrapped in an `{{#if prior_eval_path}}…{{/if}}` conditional so the block is omitted on attempt 1.
Handlebars.compile on the file succeeds.

tasks.title: Author .tap/prompts/REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md
acceptance: Template contains handlebars placeholders for: task_id, task_title, task_files, task_acceptance, specs_path, contract_path, eval_path.
Template explicitly instructs writing EVAL_RESULT.md at {{eval_path}} via the Write tool, with the verdict/rationale/issues block format shown inline.
Triple-brace `{{{ }}}` applied to user-text placeholders (task_title, task_files items, task_acceptance items); double-brace for controlled IDs/paths.
Template ends with an explicit kickoff directive.
Handlebars.compile on the file succeeds.

stories.title: ContextEngine service
acceptance: ContextEngine is an Effect Tag with a Live layer that renders both role contracts from the two prompt templates using handlebars.
Missing placeholder values raise a tagged TemplateRenderFailed error rather than silently leaving a blank.

tasks.title: Implement ContextEngine.ts
acceptance: Exports an Effect Tag `ContextEngine` with methods `renderComposer(input: ComposerRenderInput): Effect<string, TemplateRenderError>` and `renderReviewer(input: ReviewerRenderInput): Effect<string, TemplateRenderError>`.
Exposes a pure factory `makeContextEngine(composerSrc: string, reviewerSrc: string): ContextEngine["Type"]` so tests can inject custom template sources without touching disk.
`ContextEngineLive` layer reads the two templates from `.tap/prompts/COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md` + `REVIEWER_CONTRACT.md` via `@effect/platform` FileSystem and delegates to `makeContextEngine`.

Renders with handlebars `strict: true`; when Handlebars throws for an undefined placeholder, the error is translated into a `TemplateRenderError` with `missingKey` extracted from the throw message (pattern: `"<name>" not defined`).

Internal translators `toComposerContext` / `toReviewerContext` flatten camelCase RenderInput into the snake_case handlebars context (feature_goal, feature_constraints, task_id, task_title, task_files, task_acceptance, specs_path, contract_path, prior_eval_path, git_status, eval_path) — with `Option.getOrElse(priorEval, () => "")` for absence so no null/undefined leaks into the template context.
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes.

tasks.title: Tests for ContextEngine
acceptance: Test file lives at `src/services/__tests__/ContextEngine.test.ts` (plural `__tests__/` per CLAUDE.md; amends the earlier singular path in this contract).

Fixture Task + Feature rendered via the PRODUCTION `.tap/prompts/COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md` source asserts substring presence of every user-provided value verbatim (feature goal, each constraint, task id/title, each file, each acceptance criterion, specs path, contract path, git status).

Missing-placeholder case uses `makeContextEngine` with a custom template string that references an undefined key (e.g. `{{ mystery_field }}`); the Effect fails with `{ _tag: "TemplateRenderFailed", template: "COMPOSER_CONTRACT.md", missingKey: "mystery_field" }`.

User-text-contains-`{{`-braces case sets `task.title` and a `task.acceptance` entry to strings containing literal `{{ ... }}` fragments; the rendered output contains those fragments verbatim (triple-brace in the template prevents re-interpretation).
Reviewer render smoke asserts the `evalPath` appears verbatim and the inline `<eval:verdict>` schema fragment is present in the rendered output.
No `any`, no `as unknown as`; brand-construction uses a single narrow `brand<B>(s)` helper with one `as` cast.
`bun test src/services/__tests__/ContextEngine.test.ts` passes; the full `bun test` suite also passes.

stories.title: EvalParser service
acceptance: EvalParser reads the contents of an EVAL_RESULT.md file and returns a structured EvalResult or a tagged EvalParseFailed.
Parser tolerates surrounding prose outside the three XML-tagged blocks but requires all three to be present.

tasks.title: Implement EvalParser.ts
acceptance: Exports an Effect Tag `EvalParser` with method `parse(rawContent: string): Effect<EvalResult, EvalParseFailed>` — error channel narrowed from the d.ts-declared `EvalParseError` umbrella because `EvalResultMissing` belongs to the caller's file-read step (RunTask), not to pure parsing of already-read content.
Exposes a pure `parseEvalContent(rawContent)` function (used by `EvalParserLive` via `Layer.succeed`) so tests share one implementation with the service layer.

Extracts <eval:verdict>, <eval:rationale>, <eval:issues> blocks via regex (non-greedy body match); parses the issues YAML with the `yaml` npm lib (v2.8.3) into EvalIssue[]; maps snake_case YAML keys (`acceptance_failed`, `suggested_fix`) to the camelCase EvalIssue shape; brands `file` as `AbsolutePath` at the parse boundary.
Tolerates surrounding prose outside the three XML blocks; requires all three tags present or raises `EvalParseFailed` with the raw content preserved.
Accepts empty <eval:issues> when verdict is PASS; requires at least one issue when verdict is FAIL (PASS-or-has-issues invariant).
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes.

tasks.title: Tests for EvalParser
acceptance: Test file lives at `src/services/__tests__/EvalParser.test.ts` (plural `__tests__/` per CLAUDE.md; amends the earlier singular path in this contract, matching the S3.T2 precedent).
Fixture: PASS with empty issues → parses to { verdict: 'PASS', issues: [] }.

Fixture: FAIL with two issues → parses to the two-issue array with correct field mapping, including snake_case → camelCase (`acceptance_failed` → `acceptanceFailed`, `suggested_fix` → `suggestedFix`).
Fixture: missing verdict tag → raises EvalParseFailed with a helpful `reason` mentioning 'verdict' and rawContent preserved verbatim.
Fixture: malformed YAML in <eval:issues> (unterminated string) → raises EvalParseFailed with the raw content preserved.
Fixture: FAIL with empty issues → raises EvalParseFailed with reason mentioning 'FAIL' (violates the PASS-or-has-issues invariant).
No `any`, no `as unknown as`; brand-construction uses a single narrow `brand<B>(s)` helper with one `as` cast, matching the ContextEngine.test.ts convention.
`bun test src/services/__tests__/EvalParser.test.ts` passes; full `bun test` suite also passes (9 tests).

stories.title: AgentRunner service
acceptance: AgentRunnerLive spawns `claude -p --agent <role> --output-format stream-json --verbose --dangerously-skip-permissions`, pipes stdin, tees stdout to the jsonl log and stderr to the stderr log, and yields a `Stream<AgentEvent>` alongside the final `result` event.
AgentRunnerEcho layer is a deterministic fake for tests, driven by an in-memory script and capable of writing a canned EVAL_RESULT.md when the Reviewer role is invoked.
Neither layer uses `detached: true`.

tasks.title: Implement AgentRunnerLive
acceptance: Uses `@effect/platform` Command + CommandExecutor from `@effect/platform-bun` to spawn the fixed command with stdin fed from AgentRunOptions.stdin.
Parses stdout line-by-line as NDJSON, decodes each line into AgentEvent via `effect/Schema`, tees raw lines to AgentRunOptions.logPath.
Tees stderr to AgentRunOptions.stderrLogPath.
Never passes `detached: true`. On Effect interruption, sends SIGTERM to the child process via the Command API.

Exposes `AgentRunner` Tag with method `run(opts: AgentRunOptions): Effect<{ events: AgentEvent[]; result: AgentEvent & { type: 'result' } }, ComposerSpawnFailed | ReviewerSpawnFailed | ComposerMaxTurnsExceeded | ReviewerMaxTurnsExceeded | FilesystemError>`.
Maps `result.subtype === 'error_max_turns'` to the role-specific MaxTurnsExceeded tagged error.
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes.

tasks.title: Implement AgentRunnerEcho fake layer
acceptance: Exports `AgentRunnerEcho(script)` returning a `Layer<AgentRunner>` where `script` is `{ Composer: { events: AgentEvent[]; exit: { _tag: 'ok' | 'maxTurns' | 'spawnFail' } }; Reviewer: same + evalFileContent?: string }`.
When invoked with role='Reviewer' and `evalFileContent` is present, writes that string to AgentRunOptions.evalPath via FileSystem before emitting the scripted result.
Respects the scripted exit and returns the matching tagged error when not `ok`.
Does not spawn any real process.
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes.

tasks.title: Tests for AgentRunner (Echo + schema roundtrip)
acceptance: Roundtrip test: a canned NDJSON fixture of system/assistant/tool_use/tool_result/result events decodes into the AgentEvent union without loss of known fields.
Echo test: scripted Composer `ok` then scripted Reviewer that writes EVAL_RESULT.md produces the expected log files on disk and yields the `result` event in both cases.
Echo test for `error_max_turns` exit yields the role-specific MaxTurnsExceeded tagged error.
`bun test src/services/__test__/AgentRunner.test.ts` passes.
AgentRunnerLive is NOT exercised in this test file — it is manual/integration only.

stories.title: RunTask orchestration (the vertical slice)
acceptance: `runTask(task, feature, paths)` returns a TaskResult on PASS and a tagged RunTaskError on any failure in the pipeline.
The full PASS → TaskResult path is demonstrated by an integration test using AgentRunnerEcho + real ContextEngine + real EvalParser.
The four failure paths (Composer max turns, Composer spawn fail, Reviewer eval missing, Reviewer eval parse fail) each surface the correct tagged error.

tasks.title: Implement runTask orchestration
acceptance: Effect signature: `runTask(task: Task, feature: Feature, paths: { featureRoot: string; specsPath: string; contractPath: string; attempt: number }): Effect<TaskResult, RunTaskError, AgentRunner | ContextEngine | EvalParser | FileSystem>`.

Steps in order: compute log paths + eval path from `paths` and `attempt`; ensure log directory exists; render Composer contract; invoke AgentRunner for Composer; render Reviewer contract (with eval_path placeholder filled); invoke AgentRunner for Reviewer; read EVAL_RESULT.md via FileSystem (raise EvalResultMissing if absent); parse with EvalParser; assemble TaskResult with durationMs measured via Clock.
Every service error is mapped into the RunTaskError tagged union; no raw Effect errors leak out.
`bunx tsc --noEmit` passes.

tasks.title: Integration test for runTask
acceptance: PASS fixture: Composer scripted 'ok' + Reviewer scripted with a canned PASS EVAL_RESULT.md yields a TaskResult with verdict='PASS', issues=[], and all three log/eval paths existing on disk.
FAIL fixture: Reviewer scripted with a canned FAIL EVAL_RESULT.md (two issues) yields a TaskResult with verdict='FAIL' and the two EvalIssues correctly mapped.
Composer max-turns fixture yields `ComposerMaxTurnsExceeded` — Reviewer is never invoked.
Reviewer returns 'ok' but no EVAL_RESULT.md on disk → yields `EvalResultMissing` with the expected path.
Reviewer writes malformed EVAL_RESULT.md → yields `EvalParseFailed` with `rawContent` populated.
`bun test src/services/__test__/RunTask.test.ts` passes.
